jobs.description: # Shopify Tracking Specialist Needed — Server-Side GTM, Meta CAPI, GA4 & Google Ads Audit ## About the project We run a Shopify store (Moonglow Australia — personalized moon phase jewelry) and need a specialist to upgrade our tracking infrastructure end-to-end. Our current setup is functional but basic: native Shopify-Meta CAPI, multiple duplicate Google tags flagged by Tag Assistant, and no server-side GTM. We want this rebuilt properly, once, by someone who knows what they're doing. ## What we currently have - Shopify store with Facebook & Instagram app (Maximum data sharing — native CAPI active) - Meta Pixel installed and firing (single pixel, confirmed via Meta Pixel Helper) - Google Ads + Merchant Center connected and active (PMAX running) - GA4 receiving traffic - **Multiple duplicate Google tags flagged in Tag Assistant** — Shopify is showing a yellow warning to clean these up - No server-side GTM container yet ## Scope of work **Server-side infrastructure** - Set up server-side Google Tag Manager via Stape - Configure custom tracking subdomain (e.g., data.moonglow.com.au) for first-party cookies - Web GTM container properly linked to the server container **Meta** - Implement Meta Conversions API via server-side GTM - Replace or properly supplement the existing Shopify-native CAPI - Event deduplication between browser Pixel and CAPI using event_id - Advanced Matching with hashed customer parameters (email, phone, fbp, fbc, external_id, IP, UA) - All standard ecommerce events: PageView, ViewContent, AddToCart, InitiateCheckout, AddPaymentInfo, Purchase - Target Event Match Quality of 8+/10 - Verify in Events Manager that events show "Browser and Server" with proper deduplication **Google** - Audit and remove the duplicate Google tags currently flagged in Shopify - Implement Google Ads Enhanced Conversions server-side - Audit and properly configure GA4 events (purchase, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, view_item, etc.) with full ecommerce parameters - GA4 measurement protocol via server-side for ad-blocker resilience - Conversion linker properly configured ## Deliverables - Fully working server-side tracking setup as described above - Written documentation of what's been configured (containers, triggers, variables, tags) - Loom walkthrough of the GTM web + server containers - Verification screenshots: - Meta Events Manager (showing browser + server, deduplication, EMQ score) - Google Tag Assistant (confirming no duplicates) - GA4 DebugView (events firing with parameters) - Google Ads conversion diagnostics - 30 days of post-launch support for any issues that come up ## What to include in your proposal 1. Brief description of your experience with **Shopify + Stape specifically** (not just generic GTM) 2. One example of a previous project: ideally showing before/after Event Match Quality scores or attribution improvements 3. Your approach to event deduplication between browser and server 4. Estimated timeline 5. Fixed-price quote for the full scope above ## What we're looking for - Demonstrated Shopify + Stape experience — please don't apply if your experience is only with WooCommerce or custom sites - You proactively mention deduplication, event_id matching, and first-party cookies in your proposal - Clean communication, fixed-price, single milestone via Upwork escrow ## Budget No fixed budget set. Please quote based on the full scope and your approach. We're evaluating proposals on quality and fit, not lowest price — so quote what the work is worth to do properly. Payment: single milestone via Upwork escrow. We fund the full amount up-front and release on verification. ## Timeline Looking to start within 1–2 weeks. Project should take 1–2 weeks of elapsed time to complete.
jobs.title: Shopify Tracking Specialist Needed — Server-Side GTM, Meta CAPI, GA4 & Google Ads Audit

jobs.description: Project Overview I operate an established Shopify store selling custom wood shelving. Products are custom-sized to order with pricing that varies across 6 configuration dimensions. I have a complete, clean pricing database of 68,640 exact price points and fully documented business rules. I am looking for an experienced Shopify developer to build a unified order form section that is embedded across all product pages, with real-time pricing driven by a secure server-side database lookup. This is a well-defined, well-documented project. The data work is done. The business rules are documented. Mockups of the desired order form are included. What is needed is precise technical implementation. What Needs to Be Built 1. Unified Order Form Section A single Shopify Online Store 2.0 app block embedded across all product pages. The section reads the current page’s material type as the default selection but exposes all 10 material types so the customer can switch without leaving the page. One codebase, one API connection, one validation layer — no duplication across product pages. 2. Product Configurator UI The order form presents the following options in a clean logical sequence: • Material: dropdown defaulting to current page material, all 10 selectable • Finish: Finished or Unfinished — availability depends on material (see rules below) • Product Type: Slab (no hardware) or Shelf with Hardware • Thickness: 0.5”, 0.75”, 1”, 1.5” (coming soon — slab only), 1.75”, 2”, 2.1–4”, 4.1–6” (slab only) • Width: two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches 9–96 plus 1/16th fraction • Depth: two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches plus 1/16th fraction, maps to one of 6 pricing ranges 3. Real-Time Dynamic Pricing As the customer changes any option the displayed price updates instantly. Pricing is a direct server-side database lookup — no formulas, no client-side exposure, no approximations. Prices display as whole numbers. The price the customer sees is exactly what they pay at checkout. 4. Availability and Validation Rules Finish availability by material: Materials Finish Options Notes 1, 2, 3 Finished and Unfinished Both available 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 Finished only No unfinished option 10 Unfinished only No finished option Shelf with hardware availability: Depth Width Range Shelf with Hardware? 3–6" 9–96" Yes 6.1–12" 9–96" Yes 12.1–16" 9–96" Yes 16.1–20" Any No — slab only 20.1–24" Any No — slab only 24.1–28" Any No — slab only Depth pricing ranges: Customer Enters Depth Used for Pricing 3" to 6" 3–6 6.1" to 12" 6.1–12 12.1" to 16" 12.1–16 16.1" to 20" 16.1–20 20.1" to 24" 20.1–24 24.1" to 28" 24.1–28 Dimension Input Rules Width Two coordinated dropdowns — primary whole inches (9–96) and secondary fractional inches in 1/16th inch increments (1/16 through 15/16). Fraction rule: any fraction present rounds UP to the next whole inch for pricing. Example: 30 and 1/8" looks up the price for width 31". Ceiling rule: if 96" is selected, the fraction dropdown must be disabled — no fraction permitted at maximum width. Depth Two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches plus 1/16th fraction — within the selected pricing range. Fraction rule: any fraction rounds UP to next whole inch then maps to the appropriate pricing range. Example: 6 and 1/16" rounds up to 7", maps to 6.1–12 range. Ceiling rule: 28" is the maximum depth. If 28" is selected the fraction dropdown must be disabled. Thickness Option Type Pricing Fraction Input 0.5", 0.75", 1", 1.5" Coming soon Placeholder price — hide or mark coming soon in UI Not applicable until live 1.75" Fixed Always prices as 1.75" Not applicable 2" Fixed Always prices as 2" Not applicable 2.1–4" Range All values price as "2.1-4" regardless of exact measurement Shown for order record. Disabled at 4" ceiling. 4.1–6" Range All values price as "4.1-6" regardless of exact measurement Shown for order record. Disabled at 6" ceiling. Ceiling validation summary: Dimension Maximum Rule Width 96" Fraction dropdown disabled at 96" Depth 28" Fraction dropdown disabled at 28" Thickness 2.1–4" range 4" Fraction dropdown disabled at 4" Thickness 4.1–6" range 6" Fraction dropdown disabled at 6" General rule: fractions entered by the customer are recorded on the order for manufacturing purposes but do not affect pricing beyond the round-up rule above. Data Provided to Developer The following files will be provided upon engagement: • slab_pricing_complete.csv — 68,640 price records with columns: material, finish, product_type, hardware, thickness, depth, width_inches, price, placeholder • slab_pricing_schema.sql — ready-to-run SQL with CREATE TABLE statement, lookup index, and all INSERT statements • Two order form mockup PDFs showing the proposed UI layout including open dropdown states Price lookup query: SELECT price FROM slab_pricing WHERE material = ? AND finish = ? AND product_type = ? AND hardware = ? AND thickness = ? AND depth = ? AND width_inches = ? LIMIT 1; The placeholder column flags the four coming soon thickness options (0.5", 0.75", 1", 1.5") with placeholder = yes and price = $1,967. These should be hidden or marked coming soon until real pricing is available. The architecture must support a simple CSV import to activate them when ready. Technical Environment • Platform: Shopify (existing live store, Online Store 2.0) • Architecture: OS 2.0 app block embedded across all product pages • Database: MySQL — schema and complete data provided and ready to import • API: lightweight server-side endpoint — Node.js, Python, or Cloudflare Workers all acceptable • Pricing data must not be exposed client-side Deliverables 1. Unified order form section (OS 2.0 app block) embedded across all product pages, defaulting to current page material, with all 6 configuration options correctly presented and validated 2. Real-time price display updating correctly as options change, driven by server-side database lookup 3. All availability and validation rules enforced — options hidden or disabled per the rules documented above 4. Correct price passed through to cart and checkout — not a frontend estimate but the actual looked-up value 5. Password-protected CSV import tool allowing pricing to be updated by uploading a new CSV file — complete database replacement in one operation, no developer involvement required for routine price updates 6. Technical QA — Liquid code review by our CMO, checking for native sections, zero page builder dependencies, clean template structure 2. Visual approval — founder sign-off on staging Payment at each milestone releases only when both sign-offs are complete. *Milestones:* 1. Theme setup + homepage 2. jobs.description: Hey there 👋 First, let me introduce myself. I'm the founder of BitBrahma — a small tech brand from Bengaluru that sells refurbished laptops and mobile parts. The name means "Creator of Computing" and honestly, right now the only thing I haven't been able to create is... my own website. 😅 I have the products. I have the brand. I have the logo. I even have the domain — bitbrahma.com — just sitting there, waiting, judging me silently every day. Today that changes. I want to launch. --- 🧡 What I'm Building A complete Shopify store for BitBrahma — selling refurbished laptops and mobile parts & accessories to everyday Indian buyers who want good tech without spending a fortune. This isn't a side project. This is my livelihood. So I need someone who actually cares, not just someone who copy-pastes a theme and disappears. --- 📋 What Needs to Get Done (All of It) 1. Shopify store setup on bitbrahma.com — Dawn free theme, brand colors (saffron #ff6b00, dark #1a0a00), BitBrahma logo uploaded, homepage with hero banner, 6 collection blocks, trust badges, WhatsApp in footer 2. 6 collections with proper navigation — Refurbished Laptops, Screens, Batteries, Cables & Chargers, Back Covers, Earphones & Accessories — Laptop section needs filters by brand, RAM, and processor 3. 65 products uploaded from my Excel sheet — 15 refurbished laptops (specs: processor, RAM, storage, grade, warranty) — 50 mobile parts (10 per category, tagged by phone brand & model) — I'll provide the data sheet and images. You just bring the skill. 4. Razorpay + COD payments — Razorpay integration (UPI, cards, net banking, wallets) + Cash on Delivery — Please test a dummy transaction before telling me it's done 🙏 5. Shipping setup — Free shipping above ₹999, flat ₹99 below (mobile parts) — Flat ₹199 for laptops — Shiprocket/Delhivery plugin if possible 6. WhatsApp chat button + Judge.me reviews app (both free) 7. All the important pages — About Us, Contact, Shipping Policy, Returns Policy, Privacy Policy, T&C — I'll write the content. You create and link the pages. 8. Mobile tested and fast — Works perfectly on Android and iPhone — Pages load under 3 seconds — Google PageSpeed screenshot before handover (don't be shy if it's not perfect, just show me) 9. Full handover — All logins and credentials — A short Loom video (10 mins) showing me how to add a product, edit a price, and process an order — 15 days of bug-fix support after delivery (not "ghost and pray" support — actual support 😄) --- 💰 Budget & Payment I'll be honest — ₹7,000 is what I have. It's fixed. I'm not hiding a secret budget behind this number. But I'll pay in 3 fair milestones so you're never working without security: • Milestone 1 — ₹2,500: Store live on bitbrahma.com, theme and homepage done • Milestone 2 — ₹2,500: All 65 products uploaded, payments working • Milestone 3 — ₹2,000: Training video, full handover, mobile tested ✅ Deadline: 10 days. I'll be available on WhatsApp daily. I respond fast. I won't ghost you. --- 🤝 Who I'm Looking For Honestly? Someone who has done this before and doesn't need me to explain what a collection is. Someone who takes a little pride in their work. Someone who treats my ₹7,000 like it matters — because to me, it really does. Bonus points if you: ✅ Have built at least one live Shopify store (please share the link) ✅ Have integrated Razorpay before ✅ Are based in India and understand what Indian buyers actually want ✅ Can finish in 10 days --- 📝 Before You Apply — 3 Quick Questions I'll read every proposal. But please answer these 3 things or I genuinely won't know how to evaluate you: 1. Share a live Shopify store you've built 2. Have you done Razorpay integration before? Yes / No 3. Can you deliver everything in 10 days for ₹7,000? If you've read this far — thank you. That already tells me you're someone who pays attention. That's exactly who I want building BitBrahma's first home on the internet. Let's make something good together. 🚀 — BitBrahma Founder, Bengaluru

TO APPLY Loom walkthrough (5–10 min) of a recent Shopify build where you used Claude Code. jobs.description: About the Role: Our digital agency is expanding and seeking a talented SEO & GEO Specialist to enhance our clients' search visibility across various sectors, including e-commerce, SaaS, B2B, and consumer brands. Responsibilities: 1. Develop and execute effective SEO strategies: Enhance site visibility and improve search engine rankings for clients. 2. Technical SEO: Conduct site audits, prioritize fixes (including crawl budget, Core Web Vitals, structured data, indexation, and hreflang), and collaborate with development teams to implement these changes. 3. On-Page Optimization: Create keyword maps, revamp metadata, optimize internal linking, and ensure that content aligns with search intent and best practices for Answer Engine Optimization (AEO). 4. Content Strategy: Work with writers to build topic clusters that target Featured Snippets, People Also Ask (PAA) spots, and SGE citations. 5.O ff-Page & Digital PR: Plan and oversee link-building campaigns to strengthen domain authority while avoiding potential penalties. 6. Analytics & Reporting: Track performance using GA4, Looker Studio, and Search Console; translate data into clear roadmaps and monthly reports. 7. Tool Mastery: Utilize platforms such as Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, Semrush, and ChatGPT/LLMs to identify opportunities and automate repetitive tasks. 8. Cross-Team Collaboration: Coordinate with PPC, CRO, and creative teams to ensure that organic insights inform paid and social campaigns, and vice versa. 9. Trend Spotting: Monitor algorithm updates, schema changes, and AI-generated search experiences, adapting strategies proactively to stay ahead of competitors. Requirements: 1. 2+ years hands-on SEO experience (agency or in-house). 2. Proven wins in technical SEO and content-led growth. 3. Proficiency with Search Console, GA4, Screaming Frog, Ahrefs/Semrush, and log-file or server-log analysis. 4. Working knowledge of schema.org, Core Web Vitals, international SEO (hreflang), and mobile-first best practices. 5. Comfortable using LLMs/AI tools for keyword clustering, content briefs, or SERP analysis. 6. Strong analytical skills. 7. Excellent written and verbal communication in English. 8. HTML/CSS familiarity, experience with Shopify or WordPress, or a history of outranking household-name competitors. About Us: We are a trailblazing digital agency committed to helping brands achieve exceptional growth. We combine creativity, analytics, and strategy to craft impactful campaigns that drive measurable results. With a portfolio spanning international markets, Europe, and the US, we pride ourselves on our ability to adapt to diverse markets and deliver outstanding value to our clients. What We Offer: 1. Opportunity to work with a diverse and global client base. 2. A collaborative and supportive work environment. 3. Access to cutting-edge tools and resources to elevate your creative strategies. Ready to turn organic traffic into rocket fuel? Apply now and help our clients own the SERP.

jobs.description: I have a Hetzner VPS with OpenClaw 2026.4.15 already installed. WhatsApp is linked and Discord is partially configured, but I am not satisfied with the current communication setup – it feels unreliable and not best-practice. I need an experienced OpenClaw specialist to redo and optimize the entire communication layer and then build a professional, secure and highly autonomous multi-agent system for my Amazon FBA/private-label business. Core Requirements: 1. Full best-practice communication setup ○ Redo and optimize both WhatsApp (primary channel) and Discord (backup). ○ Make it stable, user-friendly and reliable for both text and voice notes. ○ I must be able to talk directly to the CEO agent and to each individual sub-agent. 2. 5 specialized sub-agents with strict human-in-the-loop ○ Research Agent ○ Supplier Agent ○ Launch Agent ○ Marketing Agent ○ CEO Agent (orchestrator) Important: Initially the agents must only draft all external communication (emails to suppliers, proposals, negotiations, etc.). They are not allowed to send anything without my explicit approval. When I later trust the system, I want a clear, documented process for gradually increasing their autonomy (stage-by-stage). 3. Best-practice SOUL.md + IDENTITY.md for all agents (professional, direct, honest, ROI-focused, no fluff). 4. Best-practice Mission Control / Control Center ○ I want a beautiful and pragmatic organization chart exactly like this example: https://youtube.com/shorts/Tr-RW09qcho?si=1sQ-54jIaQeDRBzN (Hierarchic tree with CEO at the top, colored agent nodes below, live status, clickable cards). ○ Clickable agent cards that open a live “virtual screen” / side panel when clicked. This panel must show: § What the agent is currently working on (live task + log) § Recent actions § Internal status / memory ○ Tabs: Marketing, Supply Chain, New Products Pipeline, Overall Status, Token Usage ○ Automatic 6-hour heartbeat reports 5. LLM model recommendations – suggest and configure the optimal mix (cost vs performance). 6. All essential tools & APIs must be installed and configured ○ Amazon SP-API (full integration for orders, inventory, listings, PPC, etc.) ○ Product research tools (Jungle Scout and/or Helium 10 API or best current alternatives – with programmatic access) ○ Dedicated email accounts for the agents (separate business emails or SMTP setup) so each agent can draft and (after my approval) send emails independently. ○ Any other best-practice tools needed for a fully autonomous Amazon workflow. 7. Future webshop development and autonomous management Over time the agents should be able to autonomously develop, launch and manage a dedicated webshop for me. Recommend the optimal platform and tools (e.g. Shopify, WooCommerce or best alternative in 2026) and integrate it fully with the agents for autonomous product creation, inventory sync, order processing, SEO, marketing, etc. Important pre-kickoff requirement: Before we start the actual work, please share with me examples or mockups of how the Mission Control / Control Center dashboard will look (including the organization chart style from the YouTube link above + clickable virtual screen), and explain your recommended best practices for it. I want to see and approve the design before any coding begins. Delivery method – Stage-Gate: We work in clear milestones. I will test each phase thoroughly before we move to the next. Milestones: • Milestone 1: Redo WhatsApp + Discord to best-practice + security hardening + SOUL/IDENTITY files + dedicated agent emails • Milestone 2: Create 5 sub-agents + approved Mission Control dashboard (with organization chart + clickable virtual screens) + heartbeat system • Milestone 3: Install all APIs/tools (Amazon SP-API + research tools) + draft-only email workflow + LLM configuration • Milestone 4: Full testing, documentation, handover session and training (including webshop roadmap) What I expect: • Proven experience with OpenClaw multi-agent setups (please share examples). • Strong focus on security, human-in-the-loop design and gradual autonomy. • Good English and clear communication. • Ability to work via Zoom screen-share. If you have built similar autonomous Amazon/agent systems before, please mention it in your proposal.
jobs.title: Openclaw setup for Amazon business
